rulings.Case Title: IN RE: ROBERT DONJACOUR 2019 TRUST DATED FEBRUARY 4, 2020
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/9/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Proof of payment of the assessment fee ordered in the conservatorship case must be filed before the accounting can be approved. (2) No liabilities are shown on Schedule I, but attorney fees and costs granted on 1/2/24 do not appear to have been disbursed during this accounting period. (3) Schwab charged $62,364.72, not $31,682.36. The income generated was a little over $102,000, but losses on sales were more than double the gains on sales... (4) The settlor skis and cycles? (5) Bond was reduced to $2,534,000 as of 1/2/24. (6) Schedule F is missing the number of shares of the securities. (7) Does the settlor receive no other income? Or is other income (Social Security or pension?) managed separately? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/24/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MADELEINE J. LEONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (Report Of Sale And Petition For Order Confirming Sale Of Real Property)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SUN WAH MOON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/9/25. Examiner's Notes sent to attorney with the following issues: 1. Date of death is stated to 2/10/24. Please correct. 2. Bond is insufficient, which was fixed at $170,000 but never increased; bond must be increased before final distribution can be ordered. 3. Notice of Hearing on the amended petition has not been filed. 4. Revised proposed order has not been filed. 5. The accounting does not balance. 6. Schedule A total does not appear to match the individual items. 7. Support for extraordinary fees is missing: provide declaration and separate timesheets for extraordinary and statutory services. 8. End of account cash assets have not been verified. 9. Schedule I contains change in value of securities that consists of unrealized gain and change in market value. Unrealized gain and change in market values do not belong in the accounting. If the treasury bills have been sold, they must be listed in the schedule of gains / losses and only the carry value and sale proceeds must be used. 10. Various disbursements require further explanation and substantiation.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF PEGGY LYNCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Authorization To Return Assets To Trust, Including Conservatees Interest In Apn 249-48-012 Commonly Known As 235 E. Julian In San Jose, Ca; To Effectuate Any And All Documents As Needed
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/18/2025 at counsel's request by email request to the assigned examiner on 10/31/2025. The following issues to be cleared/addressed a week before the date: (1) Further allegations are needed regarding the petitioner's standing under Probate Code Section 850(a)(3). It must be clarified whether the petitioner is acting as a trustee or as an interested person. If the latter, please provide additional details demonstrating how the petitioner qualifies as an interested person. (2) Need allegations about whether the property is currently in someone else's possession (such as a tenant or renter). If it is, the individuals in possession must be served notice in accordance with Civil Code Procedure Section 413.10, as stipulated by Probate Code Section 851(a)(2). (3) Need information about who the successor-in-interest is for John's estate, including whether he died testate. If he did die testate, a copy of his will must be provided. (4) Need clarification that this petition is for the 50% being titled as community property by John and Peggy in the real property 233 and 235 E. Julian, or only 235 E. Julian. Deeds and Schedule A present 235 E only; however, the petition sets forth 233 and 235 E. Julian. (5) Need an authority under which the court should allow the petitioner, in which capacity, to sign the affidavit for the purpose of transferring John's shares to Peggy.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VIVIAN L MACK
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/24/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Item 2d of the petition is incomplete. If the petitioner is not the acting trustee, bond appears to be necessary. No waivers of bond have been filed. (2) Grandchild Tanika Mack was not given notice. (3) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) Item 8 of the petition is missing the decedent's daughter (who is named in the will) and her date of death. [LRSF14.18 D.1.] (5) Jonathan Elias Mackis a minor. Petitioner is presumably his father, but the petition does not disclose this. [LRSF 14.18 C.3.] How is his interest represented? (6) The will disinherits any heirs, issue, relatives, or other persons who are not named, mentioned, designated, or described in the will or the trust. Why is Petitioner not also disinherited if Ronald and his issue are? Copies of pertinent pages of the trust might need to be filed to show who the trust beneficiaries are. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/10/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MIMI WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/16/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? (2) Whose children are the niblings listed in item 8 of the petition? Item 5a(8) of the petition was checked, so they are presumably the children of a surviving sister, in which case, they are not heirs required to be listed or given notice. (3) If, however, item 5a(8) of the petition was incorrect, the decedent's sibling(s) who was/were the parent(s) of those niblings must be identified and his/her/their date(s) of death set forth. (4) If the niblings are heirs, if they are citizens of Canada, notice must be given to the Canadian consular official in the US. [Probate Code Section 8113.] Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/2/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
